englishDefinition: To make something split into pieces, often by force.
To do something better than anyone has ever done before.
To stop working, especially of a machine or vehicle.
To end a romantic relationship, or for a group to split apart.
A structure built over a river or road so people can cross.
Lasting only a short time; not long.
Full of light; shining strongly.
To return something or to make a memory come into the mind.
To raise a child, or to mention a topic in conversation.
Belonging to or coming from the United Kingdom.
To study something again to improve a skill you once had.
A round open container with a handle, used for carrying water.
To make something by putting parts or materials together.
A structure with walls and a roof, like a house or office.
To meet someone by chance without planning to.
To be on fire or to be damaged by fire or great heat.
To break open suddenly because of pressure inside.
The activity of making, buying, or selling things to earn money.
A man whose work is buying, selling, or running a company.
Having a lot to do; not free.
A soft yellow food made from milk, often spread on bread.
A small round part you press to make a machine work, or to fasten clothes.
Without planning; happening by luck.
A round leafy vegetable, often green or purple.
A piece of furniture with doors and shelves for storing things.
A thick wire used for carrying electricity or signals.
A restaurant where people serve themselves from a counter.
A box made of bars used to keep animals or birds inside.
A chart that shows the days, weeks, and months of a year.
To telephone someone again later, returning their earlier call.
Not nervous, angry, or upset; quiet and peaceful.
A device used to take photographs or record video.
A place outdoors where people stay in tents for a short time.
The land and buildings of a university or college.
A large country in northern North America.
Belonging to or coming from Canada.
To decide that a planned event will not happen.
A serious disease in which abnormal cells grow inside the body.
A stick of wax with a string in the middle that burns to give light.
A sweet food made mostly from sugar.
The leader of a sports team, ship, or aircraft.
A road vehicle with four wheels and an engine, used to carry people.
To feel concern or interest in someone or something.
The job or series of jobs a person does for a long time.
Paying full attention to avoid mistakes or danger.
Not paying enough attention; making mistakes easily.
A thick soft cover for the floor, made of cloth.
A long orange vegetable that grows underground.
To hold something and take it with you to another place.
A funny drawing or animated film, often for children.
A particular situation or example of something.
Money in the form of paper bills and metal coins.
A large strong building with high walls, where kings or queens lived long ago.
To take hold of something moving through the air with your hands.
To make something happen, especially something bad.
The top inside surface of a room.
To enjoy a special day or event with parties or activities.
A small phone you can carry and use almost anywhere.
A unit of money in many countries; one hundred cents make one dollar.
The middle point or part of something.
A unit for measuring length; one hundred of them make a meter.
Located in the middle of a place or area.
A period of one hundred years.
Breakfast food made from grains, often eaten with milk.
Sure that something is true or will happen.
Definitely; without any doubt.

A soft white stick used to write on a blackboard.
Something difficult that needs effort and skill to do.
An opportunity to do something, or the possibility it may happen.

To become happier, or to make someone feel happier.
Related to substances studied in chemistry, like acids or salts.
A board game for two players using sixteen pieces each.
